title: Growth Rate and Terminal Value: When calculating the terminal value in a DCF analysis, which...
summary: Assess the role of terminal value in DCF analysis as capturing value beyond the forecast period.
prompt: When calculating the terminal value in a DCF analysis, which formula is typically used for a growing perpetuity?

options.text: The formula for a growing perpetuity is PV = PMT × (1 + g) / (r - g)

solution: The formula for a growing perpetuity is PV = PMT × (1 + g) / (r - g), which calculates the present value of cash flows that grow at a constant rate indefinitely.
